rules.rule: Brokers must be thin wrappers over external resources and must contain zero business logic.
Broker interface names must be technology-agnostic (e.g., IStorageBroker, not ISqlStorageBroker).
Storage broker methods must use storage language: InsertAsync, SelectAsync, SelectAllAsync, UpdateAsync, DeleteAsync.
API broker methods must use RESTful language: PostAsync, GetAsync, PutAsync, DeleteAsync.
Queue broker methods must use queue language: EnqueueAsync, DequeueAsync.
Broker input parameters must be primitive types or native models; must not accept upstream service-layer models.
Brokers must return the raw external resource response without transformation.
Configuration values must be retrieved via IConfiguration injected through the constructor.
Brokers supporting multiple entities must be implemented as partial classes.
Broker contracts must be versioned using the SPAL strategy when breaking changes are introduced.
